title: GoalOS Signoff Pro — Proof Before Settlement Institutional Research Lab v30
subtitle: A prestigious research-to-adoption public lab for the GoalOS blockchain credibility standard.
headline: Turn the paper into the standard.
motto: Blockchain proves the transaction. GoalOS proves the work.
tagline: No Proof. No Trust. No Settlement.

researchObjects.name: Work-Verification Gap
researchObjects.plain: A blockchain can settle a transaction without proving that the off-chain work behind it was real, complete, reviewed, or accepted.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Makes the problem obvious to DAOs, auditors, grant programs, exchanges, partners, and users.

researchObjects.name: Proof Object Model
researchObjects.plain: Mission Contract, Evidence Docket, ProofBundle, Replay Log, Validator Report, Risk Ledger, Human Authority, Mission Receipt, Anchor, Chronicle.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Gives teams a shared vocabulary for credible proof packages.

researchObjects.name: Acceptance Predicate
researchObjects.plain: Settlement readiness exists only when required proof objects exist, replay is possible, validation passes, risk is bounded, and the human authority gate is explicit.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Turns vague milestone acceptance into a reviewable decision.

researchObjects.name: Settlement-Safety Invariant
researchObjects.plain: No proof package should produce settlement readiness unless its mission, evidence, replay, validation, risk, authority, receipt, and boundary gates pass.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Creates a simple rule that stakeholders can demand.

researchObjects.name: Claim-Maturity Lattice
researchObjects.plain: Claims mature from unsubmitted to submitted, internally checked, independently replayed, accepted, anchored, challenged, and chronicle-admitted.
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Prevents raw claims from masquerading as institutional proof.

researchObjects.name: Proof Mandate
researchObjects.plain: Users, DAOs, investors, auditors, enterprises, and ecosystem partners can ask one question: where is the proof package?
researchObjects.institutionalUse: Converts the research into practical market pressure.

adoptionBlueprint.name: Declare the standard
adoptionBlueprint.output: Publicly state that important blockchain work requires mission, evidence, validation, receipt, and settlement-readiness boundaries.

adoptionBlueprint.name: Require proof packages for new milestones
adoptionBlueprint.output: DAO grants, treasury payments, audits, partnerships, and AI-agent tasks must define acceptance criteria before execution.

adoptionBlueprint.name: Publish public-safe dockets
adoptionBlueprint.output: Each material claim links to a redacted Evidence Docket, replay path, validator report, risk ledger, and human signoff.

adoptionBlueprint.name: Bind receipts to governance and settlement readiness
adoptionBlueprint.output: No payout, upgrade, milestone closure, or public credibility claim without an accepted Mission Receipt.

adoptionBlueprint.name: Use proof as institutional memory
adoptionBlueprint.output: Accepted work enters the Chronicle and influences future missions only with proof provenance.

standardClauses.clause: No Proof Package, No Settlement Readiness
standardClauses.text: A milestone, grant, upgrade, audit remediation, AI-agent output, or treasury payment is not settlement-ready until the required proof package has been submitted, reviewed, and accepted.
Any material technical claim should provide a replay path, reproduction packet, or independent review route before it is used to justify governance, payment, or launch.
Automation may prepare evidence and recommend decisions, but high-impact acceptance remains bound to a named human or governance authority.
Every public proof package must state what it proves, what it does not prove, what remains unverified, and what risk remains.
Public demos and public dockets must not request personal/confidential data, use wallet integrations, move funds, or imply production settlement authority unless separately authorized.

audiences.name: DAO Delegates
audiences.ask: Where is the proof package before we vote or release treasury funds?

audiences.name: Grant Committees
audiences.ask: Which evidence proves that the milestone was earned?

audiences.name: Auditors
audiences.ask: Can the remediation claim be replayed and reviewed independently?

audiences.name: Exchanges & Partners
audiences.ask: What evidence supports the project’s credibility claim?

audiences.name: Enterprises
audiences.ask: What acceptance record proves that this vendor delivery is complete?

audiences.name: Users
audiences.ask: Why should we trust this claim before value or governance power moves?
